I haven't clogged up your feed in a while, so let's get it going again 🙃   Today, 7-18-25, we had an incredible encounter with an awesome, awe inspiring Blue whale that seemed to be a mile long. Not only was she an absolute unit, she's a mom! Enjoy this baby blue reveal.
Commencement Bay in Tacoma witnessed a rare whale appearance, a once-in-a-lifetime sight with a mere 1 percent chance. Orcas are more commonly spotted.

The video shows a fin whale performing lunge feeding. It swims fast, turns sideways, and expands its throat pleats to engulf massive amounts of water and krill. Then, it pushes water out through baleen plates, trapping the tiny crustaceans to eat. Fin whales are the second-largest species, often feeding like this on dense krill swarms. Blue whales are bigger with mottled skin and a smaller dorsal fin.
